title: Interpret AGV Obstacle Alarm Visibility In RMS
summary: Use the RMS screen and source-described AGV-to-RMS reporting behavior to understand that an AGV obstacle condition has been detected and surfaced as an alarm code in RMS.
when_to_use: Use this reference when reviewing an RMS screen to understand whether a visible AGV alarm code may represent an obstacle condition reported by the AGV's onboard hardware to RMS.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to determine corrective actions for the alarm.
Do not use this runbook to infer alarm meanings beyond the source statement that RMS generates a visible alarm code when the AGV hardware detects an obstacle.
Do not use this runbook as a troubleshooting procedure when an obstacle is suspected but no RMS alarm code is visible.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the RMS screen, identify the AGV entry, check for a visible alarm code, and interpret the alarm visibility using the source-described AGV-to-RMS reporting relationship.

safety_notes: This source describes alarm interpretation only and does not provide physical intervention steps.
Do not infer route logic, corrective actions, or hardware repair actions beyond what is stated in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Visibility into AGV alarm code display in RMS

system_preconditions: An RMS screen is available for review
The AGV or robot entry to be reviewed is visible in RMS

visual_references.description: Training frame explaining AGV versus RMS responsibilities, including obstacle reporting from AGV hardware to RMS and alarm visibility in RMS.

steps.title: View the RMS screen
steps.purpose: Access the interface where the source says AGV alarm codes are visible.
steps.instruction: Open or view the RMS screen where AGV information and alarm codes are visible.
steps.expected_result: The RMS screen is available for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The user is viewing RMS and can inspect AGV-related information.
steps.failure_condition: The RMS screen is not available or cannot be viewed.

actions.label: Open RMS
actions.exact_action: Open or view the RMS screen where AGV information and alarm codes are visible.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: RMS referenced as the management system receiving AGV-reported conditions and surfacing alarm visibility.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the RMS screen cannot be viewed, because the source does not provide access troubleshooting steps.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript states that if the AGV sees an obstacle, the hardware reports it to RMS, and RMS generates an alarm code visible on the RMS screen.

steps.title: Identify the AGV entry under review
steps.purpose: Ensure the alarm interpretation is tied to the specific AGV or robot entry being reviewed.
steps.instruction: Identify the AGV or robot entry being reviewed on the RMS screen.
steps.expected_result: A specific AGV or robot entry is selected or identified for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The user can clearly tell which AGV or robot entry is being examined in RMS.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV or robot entry cannot be identified from the available RMS view.

actions.label: Identify AGV entry
actions.exact_action: Identify the AGV or robot entry being reviewed on the RMS screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training slide context showing RMS as the place where AGV alarms are visible and where AGV-versus-RMS responsibilities are discussed.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the AGV or robot entry cannot be identified, because the source does not provide navigation or lookup instructions.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript states that if the AGV sees an obstacle, the hardware reports it to RMS, and RMS generates an alarm code visible on the RMS screen.

source_evidence.short_description: Training slide and discussion frame explaining how Geek+ automated guided vehicles are controlled by the Geek+ RMS and what functions reside on the AGV versus in the management system.

steps.title: Check for a visible alarm code
steps.purpose: Determine whether RMS is displaying an alarm code for the AGV being reviewed.
steps.instruction: Check whether an alarm code is displayed for the AGV in RMS.
steps.expected_result: The user confirms whether an alarm code is visible for the AGV.
steps.healthy_condition: An alarm code is visible when the AGV obstacle condition has been reported to RMS.
steps.failure_condition: No alarm code is visible even though an obstacle condition is believed to exist.

actions.label: Inspect alarm display
actions.exact_action: Check whether an alarm code is displayed for the AGV in RMS.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The source-supported concept that RMS generates an alarm code visible on the RMS screen when the AGV reports an obstacle condition.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the RMS screen does not show an alarm code but the AGV is believed to have detected an obstacle, because the source does not provide further troubleshooting steps.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript states that if the AGV sees an obstacle, the hardware reports it to RMS, and RMS generates an alarm code visible on the RMS screen.

steps.title: Interpret the alarm as AGV-reported obstacle detection
steps.purpose: Apply the source explanation of how obstacle detection is reported and surfaced in RMS.
steps.instruction: Interpret the displayed alarm in the context of the source statement that when the AGV physical hardware detects an obstacle in front of it, that condition is reported back to RMS and RMS generates an alarm code.
steps.expected_result: The visible RMS alarm is understood as reflecting an obstacle condition detected by AGV hardware and reported to RMS.
steps.healthy_condition: The user understands the alarm as a surfaced report from AGV sensing hardware to RMS.
steps.failure_condition: The user assumes unsupported meanings, corrective actions, or alternate causes not stated in the source.

actions.label: Interpret alarm source
actions.exact_action: Interpret the displayed alarm in the context of the source statement that when the AGV physical hardware detects an obstacle in front of it, that condition is reported back to RMS and RMS generates an alarm code.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: AGV forward collision sensors and the AGV-versus-RMS responsibility explanation showing obstacle detection on the AGV and alarm generation in RMS.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop interpretation if additional alarm meaning or corrective action is needed, because the source does not provide that detail.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript states that if the AGV sees an obstacle, the hardware reports it to RMS, and RMS generates an alarm code visible on the RMS screen.

source_evidence.summary: The AGV contains physical sensors and collision sensing and reports that information to RMS. RMS manages robot tasks and sends commands, while the AGV does not hold the broader task queue logic.

steps.title: Record the interpretation boundary
steps.purpose: Document the source-supported conclusion without extending beyond the evidence.
steps.instruction: Record that the visible RMS alarm reflects a condition reported by the AGV's onboard sensing hardware rather than route logic performed on the AGV itself.
steps.expected_result: The interpretation is documented in a source-consistent way.
steps.healthy_condition: The record states that the alarm reflects AGV hardware reporting to RMS and does not add unsupported diagnosis or remediation.
steps.failure_condition: The record attributes unsupported alarm meanings, route decisions, or corrective actions not present in the source.

actions.label: Document source-based interpretation
actions.exact_action: Record that the visible RMS alarm reflects a condition reported by the AGV's onboard sensing hardware rather than route logic performed on the AGV itself.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The division of responsibilities between AGV onboard sensing and RMS supervisory control.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if a corrective action or deeper troubleshooting path is required, because the source does not provide one.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript explains that inside the AGV are sensors and collision sensing, which report back to RMS, while RMS dictates what the robot needs to do and sends commands.

source_evidence.summary: When an AGV detects an obstacle, it reports the condition to RMS. RMS generates an alarm code that is visible to users looking at the RMS screen.

success_criteria: The user can recognize that an obstacle detected by AGV hardware is surfaced as an alarm code on the RMS screen.
The user can distinguish AGV onboard sensing responsibility from RMS alarm presentation responsibility.
healthy_conditions: RMS is viewable for the AGV under review.
A visible alarm code can be checked on the RMS screen.
The alarm is interpreted only within the source-supported AGV-to-RMS reporting explanation.
failure_conditions: The RMS screen cannot be viewed.
The AGV entry cannot be identified on the RMS screen.
No alarm code is visible when an obstacle condition is believed to exist.
The user infers unsupported alarm meanings or corrective actions beyond the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the RMS screen does not show an alarm code but the AGV is believed to have detected an obstacle, because the source does not provide further troubleshooting steps.
Escalate if access to RMS or the relevant AGV view is unavailable.
Escalate if corrective action, alarm code decoding, or remediation is required, because the source only supports interpretation of alarm visibility and reporting path.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training explanation of AGV obstacle sensing, reporting to RMS, and RMS alarm visibility.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Transcript states that if the AGV sees an obstacle, the hardware reports it to RMS, and RMS generates an alarm code visible on the RMS screen.

missing_details: The source does not provide specific alarm code values or a code list.
The source does not provide corrective actions for obstacle alarms.
The source does not provide troubleshooting steps for cases where an obstacle is suspected but no alarm is visible in RMS.
The source does not provide a time estimate for this review.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required.
